title: Charred Molten Oozy Gyoza Chawanmushi
description: A meal-prep Japanese dish featuring charred gyoza and a mother-sauce-base for chawanmushi, prepared in potatoe buns, suitable for ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly diets.

ingredients.name: Charred gyoza fillings (adjust based on preference)

instructions: Mix together potato flour, soy sauce, minced garlic, and black pepper in a bowl.
Add salt to taste and mix well until the mixture is smooth.
Heat butter in a small pan over medium heat. Pour in the chawanmushi base mixture and stir until fully incorporated into the butter.
Let it simmer for about 2-3 minutes or until slightly thickened, stirring occasionally.
In separate bowls, beat one egg lightly and pour it gently into the simmering chawanmushi mixture to create a smooth consistency.
Fold in charred gyoza fillings evenly across the chawanmushi base.
Carefully place folded bun wrappers over each cup of chawanmushi. Ensure no air pockets are formed during assembly.
Cover the assembled cups with plastic wrap or lid, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving to allow the gyoza fillings to set slightly.
Refrigerated portions can be stored in an airtight container for up to one week.
